title: 'Stored at -4°C, requires -80°C': Cops 'request' family to take Twisha Sharma's 'decomposing' body
content: A Bhopal family faces a grim dilemma: claim their daughter's body or pursue a second post-mortem amid decomposition fears. Twisha Sharma, 33, was found dead five months after her wedding. Her parents suspect foul play and demand an independent autopsy, but the mortuary's limited preservation facilities raise concerns about vital evidence.

title: ‘Frustration manifesting in his temperament’: BJP attacks Rahul over ‘traitor’ remark on PM
content: BJP President J.P. Nadda slammed Rahul Gandhi for calling PM Modi and Amit Shah 'traitors' who 'sold India'. Nadda attributed Gandhi's remarks to electoral frustration, questioning if fighting Naxalism or protecting India's territory constitutes treason. Gandhi had earlier accused the BJP of attacking the Constitution and weakening public institutions.

title: After five humbling defeats, Shubman Gill and co. to ditch raging turners at home
content: After debacles in home Tests against New Zealand and South Africa, India are set to move away from raging turners and return to traditional pitches with gradual wear and tear. Carefully selected venues and black/mixed soil surfaces are expected to shape India’s strategy. India will play Afghanistan in a one-off Test match from June 6.

title: 'What's wrong with it?' On caste census, SC says govt should know the numbers
content: The Supreme Court has upheld the Centre's decision to conduct caste enumeration in the upcoming 2027 Census, stating there is "nothing wrong with it." A bench led by the Chief Justice emphasized that such data is crucial for the government to identify backward classes and implement effective welfare measures. This marks the first comprehensive caste count since 1931.

title: As LinkedIn layoff emails go, employees in this US state reportedly most impacted
content: Microsoft-owned LinkedIn is implementing another wave of job cuts, with over 600 employees set to be laid off this summer, primarily impacting California. This follows an earlier reduction of 5% of its global workforce. Despite reporting revenue growth, the company cites the need to 'reinvent how we work' and fund future investments as reasons for these significant workforce adjustments.

title: India plans to send oil tankers through Hormuz for new supply amid Iran conflict disruptions
content: India is preparing to send ships through the Strait of Hormuz to secure vital crude oil and energy cargoes amid ongoing disruptions. The government is finalizing plans for Indian vessels to navigate the waterway, which handles a significant portion of global oil flows, as shipping activity has slowed due to the Iran conflict.

title: Goenka's advice to Sooryavanshi: 'Preserve innocence… the world will be at your feet'
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive 93 off 38 balls powered Rajasthan Royals to a seven-wicket victory over Lucknow Super Giants in IPL 2026. The teenage sensation's remarkable knock, featuring 10 sixes, saw him become the first Indian to hit 50 sixes in a season. LSG owner Sanjiv Goenka personally congratulated the young star, acknowledging his immense talent and maturity.

title: AWS CEO just told engineers: We will need tons and tons of software developers
content: AWS CEO Matt Garman reassures that AI won't eliminate software engineers but will transform their roles. He emphasizes a shift from basic coding to system architecture and problem-solving. Despite recent layoffs, Amazon's demand for developers is accelerating, with plans to hire thousands of interns and entry-level engineers globally, focusing on new skillsets for next-gen systems.

title: 'Economic storm brewing & ...': Rahul takes 'nautanki' swipe at PM 's gift to Meloni
content: Rahul Gandhi criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi for gifting toffees to Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni in Rome, while accusing him of "laughing and filming reels" amid the struggles of farmers, youth, women, workers, and small traders in India. Gandhi deemed the gesture "a farce" and not leadership, contrasting it with an "economic storm" brewing domestically.

title: Meta layoffs: 4am email ‘horror’ starts from Singapore
content: Meta Platforms has initiated global layoffs, with Singapore employees receiving termination notices early Wednesday. Thousands worldwide are affected as the company restructures, prioritizing AI.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg is making AI the top focus, investing heavily, while employees and investors voice concerns over data privacy and massive AI expenditure.

title: 'No work for 3 years': UP BJP councillor shaves her head in protest
content: A BJP councillor in Firozabad shaved her head at the municipal corporation to protest alleged discrimination in development works and neglect of civic issues in her ward. Usha Devi Shankhwar claims the mayor's husband is obstructing projects, a charge the Municipal Commissioner denies, stating significant work is underway.

title: Watch: PM Modi's surprise gift for Giorgia Meloni has a very 'Melody' twist
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni shared a video thanking Prime Minister Narendra Modi for gifting her 'Melody' chocolates. The term, a blend of their surnames, gained popularity during their previous meetings. This latest viral moment occurred during Modi's visit to Rome, focused on strengthening India-Italy ties across various sectors.

title: Fact check: Was Aaron Rodgers seen with wife after Steelers contract?
content: Aaron Rodgers, the NFL veteran, recently signed a one year deal with the Pittsburgh Steelers, as retirement rumors dominated the headlines, sparking chaos among millions of fans. Amid all the speculations, the spotlight remains on Aaron Rodgers’ wife even as he continues to maintain privacy about his marital life. Through all of this, a picture of Aaron Rodgers with a woman has convinced millions that the identity of his wife has finally been revealed.

title: Ronda Rousey’s net worth 2026: Earnings, endorsements and lifestyle
content: Ronda Rousey, a trailblazer in combat sports, is projected to have a net worth of nearly $14 million by 2026. Her fortune stems from lucrative UFC and WWE careers, acting, endorsements, and book sales. Rousey's impact on women's MMA and her transition to WWE cemented her status as a crossover star, ensuring her continued relevance and earning potential.

title: Oilers linked to Alex Tuch amid NHL free agency contract rumors
content: NHL trade rumors are heating up as free agency approaches, and Alex Tuch of the Buffalo Sabres is reportedly in the Edmonton Oilers’ crosshairs. Recent rumors suggest Tuch’s contract value is taking a hit due to his inconsistent play in the playoffs. It’s an opportunity for Edmonton, who are searching for top-six scoring depth and tight salary cap restrictions and roster restructuring needs.

title: ‘We are humans too, we make mistakes’: Parag urges IPL commentators to ‘talk about cricket’
content: Rajasthan Royals skipper Riyan Parag urged commentators and experts to cease personal criticism and focus solely on cricket. He emphasised players' hard work and preparation, highlighting that setbacks don't negate their efforts. Parag stressed the importance of respecting the sport and its athletes, advocating for discussions centered on the game itself.

title: Insurance company delaying your claim? Here is what IRDAI says they must do
content: In a bid to enhance policyholder rights, insurance companies are required to resolve claims within 30 days, while investigations for health claims should conclude in 30-45 days, and for life policies, within 90 days. Failure to meet these deadlines results in interest penalties.

title: 'He’s one of the main contenders': Ponting backs Iyer as future T20I captain
content: Ricky Ponting, Punjab Kings coach, believes Shreyas Iyer is a strong contender for India's next T20 captain, potentially even ahead of Suryakumar Yadav. Iyer's impressive IPL 2026 season, marked by captaining his third franchise to a final, highlights his maturity and leadership potential.

title: Did regime change plans in Iran include hardliner who called to 'wipe Israel off the map'?
content: US and Israel reportedly explored a regime-change plan in Iran, centering on former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, after alleged strikes killed top officials and the Supreme Leader.  The plan, devised by Mossad, aimed to install Ahmadinejad, despite his hardline past, but collapsed after an Israeli strike on his residence. His current whereabouts are unknown.

title: Why new Ebola outbreak has put global health experts on alert again
content: The latest Ebola outbreak, caused by the rare Bundibugyo strain, highlights global disease surveillance weaknesses, even after COVID-19. With no specific vaccine and potential for silent spread, experts fear wider transmission. This outbreak underscores the need for robust public health infrastructure and rapid response to prevent localized crises from becoming global challenges.

title: 'In my 35 years of cricket...': Breathtaking Sooryavanshi floors Justin Langer
content: Teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's breathtaking 93 off 38 balls powered Rajasthan Royals to a crucial IPL 2026 win. Coach Justin Langer lauded the 15-year-old's fearless batting, noting its impact on elite bowlers. Sooryavanshi also became the first Indian to hit 50 sixes in a season, showcasing immense potential for the future.

title: Indian Railways eyes 350 kmph indigenous bullet train; first to run at 280 kmph potential
content: India’s first bullet train is currently being manufactured by Bengaluru-based BEML and is likely to be ready by 2027.

title: 'Wants to function like family': TVK's unity pitch amid alliance strain over AIADMK rebels
content: Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay desires a unified cabinet, including supporting parties, amidst alliance strains.  Cracks appeared as CPM threatened to withdraw support if rebel AIADMK MLAs are inducted, citing a mandate for clean governance.  The TVK government, formed with outside support, faces a crucial decision on cabinet expansion.

title: Youngest and fastest ever! Vaibhav Sooryavanshi rewrites IPL history again
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues his sensational IPL 2026 run, smashing 93 off 38 balls against LSG. He became the youngest to 500 runs and set a new strike rate record. Sooryavanshi also broke the Indian six-hitting record for a season, reaching 53. His explosive innings powered Rajasthan's successful chase of 221.

title: India's S-400 shield on track: Russian deliveries remain on schedule
content: Russia's S-400 missile defense system deliveries to India are proceeding as scheduled, bolstering New Delhi's aerial shield with an additional squadron. This advanced system, a key component of India's air defense, is recognized for its precision and lethal accuracy in engaging various aerial threats.

title: Blunt injuries on body, hanging with gymnastic ring rope: What Twisha Sharma’s autopsy revealed
content: A post-mortem revealed Twisha Sharma died from hanging, but also showed blunt-force injuries. Her family alleges harassment, while the accused's mother claims Twisha sought an abortion. Police are actively searching for the prime accused, Samarth Singh, with a reward offered for information.

title: Car ride, dinner, visit to iconic Colosseum: 'Melodi' moments from Rome ahead of talks
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi met Italian counterpart Giorgia Meloni for dinner and a walk through the Colosseum upon his arrival in Rome. The leaders exchanged perspectives on boosting India-Italy friendship, with their discussions taking on a relaxed tone against the historic backdrop. Modi also received a warm welcome from the Indian diaspora in Italy.

title: How Canada-based ‘suitor’ planned Punjabi singer's murder, fled India the same night
content: Punjabi singer Inder Kaur was allegedly murdered in a chilling plot orchestrated by a Canada-based suitor who traveled to India via Nepal. Police recovered her decomposed body from a canal six days after her abduction, suspecting she was shot inside her car and then dumped. The prime accused, Sukhwinder Singh, allegedly targeted her due to marriage and financial disputes.

title: IPL playoff race: What RR, PBKS, CSK, DC and KKR need to do to grab the final spot
content: The IPL playoff race intensifies with three spots taken. Rajasthan Royals are in pole position for the final berth, needing just one win. Punjab Kings, Chennai Super Kings, Delhi Capitals, and Kolkata Knight Riders face tougher paths, requiring victories and favourable results from other matches to advance. Net run rate could prove crucial for several contenders.

title: What the new labour code actually says about working four days a week
content: In a groundbreaking update, India's new labour code paves the way for a four-day workweek, maintaining the traditional total of 48 hours per week. Employees may take on as many as 12 hours each day, though this option remains entirely optional, leaving overtime policies intact.

title: Left vs BJP in Kerala over full 'Vande Mataram' recital at Satheesan's oath ceremony
content: A political controversy has ignited in Kerala following the full recitation of 'Vande Mataram' at a new cabinet's oath-taking. The CPI(M) criticizes the move, citing historical precedents and concerns for pluralism. The BJP retorts, accusing the CPI(M) of insulting the national song and appeasing certain groups. A similar protocol dispute occurred in Tamil Nadu over song sequencing.

title: 'Forced to eat beef': Woman alleges ‘love jihad’, claims she was barred from Hindu worship
content: A 27-year-old Hindu woman in Ulhasnagar has accused Imran Sheikh of 'love jihad', alleging he concealed his Muslim identity to marry her and subsequently forced her to adopt Islamic customs. Sheikh and two of his brothers have been arrested for cheating and hurting religious sentiments.

title: Bangladesh complete historic 2-0 Test whitewash over Pakistan with 78-run win
content: Bangladesh secured a dominant 2-0 Test series whitewash against Pakistan, winning the second Test by 78 runs. Litton Das's century and strong bowling performances from Taijul Islam, who took nine wickets in the match, were pivotal to the hosts' historic victory.

title: TMC candidate Jahangir Khan's name will remain on EVMs - here's why
content: Trinamool Congress's Falta candidate, Jahangir Khan, announced he would not contest the May 21 repoll, citing the need for Falta's peace and development. This decision came after widespread electoral malpractice led to the repoll. The TMC stated Khan's withdrawal was a personal choice, while the BJP dismissed it as a tactic due to lack of support.

title: ‘Everyone knows that, but...’: Rishabh Pant drops F-bomb after LSG defeat in Jaipur
content: Lucknow Super Giants captain Rishabh Pant expressed frustration after his team's ninth loss.  He stated the team is still good despite missing the playoffs.  LSG lost to Rajasthan Royals after failing to defend a high score.  Pant acknowledged the need for experience in crucial moments.  He also defended tactical decisions made during the match.

title: PM Modi holds talks with Italian President | Live
content: PM Modi was accompanied by EAM S Jaishankar, NSA Ajit Doval, MEA Secretary (West) Sibi George and other senior diplomatic officials.

title: Constitution, voice of Gandhi, Ambedkar, under attack: Rahul in Raebareli
content: Rahul Gandhi alleged that the Prime Minister is "attacking the Constitution 24 hours a day" by weakening MNREGA, not conducting caste census, and favouring select industrialists

title: Increasing crimes against minors in Karnataka highlighted by women’s commission chairperson
content: Karnataka State Women’s Commission Chairperson Nagalakshmi Choudhary has said that crimes against minors had continue despite enacting several laws

title: Joint military exercise of 13 nations begins in Meghalaya, focus on counter-terrorism operations
content: The multilateral military exercise 'PRAGATI 2026' aims at enabling seamless coordination among participating nations in joint operations, identifying common areas of cooperation and sharing expertise

title: T.N. CM Vijay pays tribute to social reformer Pandit Iyothee Thass; launches operation of 40 new vehicles for DMRHS
content: Mr. Vijay, along with his Cabinet colleagues including Ministers N. Anand, Aadhav Arjuna, K.A. Sengottaiyan, P. Venkataramanan, R. Nirmalkumar, Rajmohan, and T.K. Prabhu, legislators, and officials, paid tributes to the social reformer

title: Delhi experiences warm morning; 'orange' alert issued for heatwave
content: An ‘orange’ alert stands for 'be prepared', while the maximum temperature is expected to hover around 46 degrees Celsius

title: KG Basin gas migration dispute: RIL informs SC its intention to write to Centre for conciliation
content: On May 19, the top court commenced final hearing on appeals of RIL and two other foreign firms challenging a Delhi High Court verdict that set aside an arbitral award in their favour in the Krishna-Godavari Basin gas migration dispute with the Centre

title: Karnataka women’s commission chairperson emphasises Implementation of safety measures
content: She said that every institution should mandatorily implement safety measures in the wake of increase in instances of sexual harassment, sexual abuse, and mental harassment against women at workplaces

title: Kolkata swelters as Meteorological department forecasts heavy rain in North Bengal
content: Heavy downpour is likely over the sub-Himalayan districts of Darjeeling, Kalimpong, Jalpaiguri, Alipurduar and Cooch Behar from May 21 to 25

title: ‘Has double-engine govt run out of oil?’ Opposition questions Maharashtra Govt over fuel shortage reports
content: Congress Legislature Party leader Vijay Wadettiwar and NCP (SP) MLA Jayant Patil cited incidents where a police vehicle sent to search for a missing girl in Nagpur apparently did not have diesel, and attack on a petrol pump worker in Jalgaon over alleged fuel shortage

title: Kerala’s UDF govt scraps K-Rail project launched by LDF
content: Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan says all land acquired for the project will be de-notified and yellow concrete markers that demarcated the prospective line will be removed

title: Twisha Sharma death: No objection to second autopsy, says Bhopal Police chief; court to examine case diary
content: The police commissioner’s statement came on the day a Judicial Magistrate First Class court is scheduled to examine the case diary on the plea moved by Twisha’s family seeking a fresh postmortem examination

title: SEIL Energy lays foundation for ₹4.5-crore inclusive sports academy for disabled in Nellore
content: Enclosed all-weather indoor stadium at the Composite Regional Centre to seat 300 and host sports for persons with physical and intellectual disabilities

title: Philanthropy Asia Summit: Asian leaders push for digital infrastructure, strong regional ties
content: The Philanthropy Asia Alliance emphasised the need to move beyond traditional, family and charity-based philanthropy towards more institutional and collaborative models for addressing common problems in Asian countries

title: SC seeks response of Centre, States on PIL seeking 30% quota for women lawyers in Govt panels
content: The plea refers to startling empirical evidence to say that while women are entering law schools in record numbers, they are systematically blocked from professional advancement

title: CM Suvendu begins first North Bengal tour, promises to fulfill region's developmental aspirations
content: The BJP government will work towards giving north Bengal its developmental dues to erase the deprivation meted out to the region earlier, says West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari, on his maiden visit to North Bengal.

title: DRDO completes final trials of ULPGM-V3 precision missile near Kurnool
content: Missile tested in air-to-ground and air-to-air modes; Bharat Dynamics and Adani Defence named as production agencies

title: India must be alert amid West Asia crisis, no immediate worry: Civil Aviation Minister Naidu
content: Kinjarapu Rammohan Naidu also announced that Jharkhand’s Dumka, Hazaribag, Chaibasa, Daltonganj and some other districts will get air connectivity soon

title: Bheemili MLA finds liquor bottles at Madhurawada health centre during surprise inspection
content: Residents tell the MLA of cattle on the grounds and people drinking on the premises at night; he orders a cleanup and night police patrols

title: ₹70 crore money laundering case: ED gets 'godman' Ashok Kharat's custody till May 26
content: The financial crime investigation agency has accused Kharat of orchestrating a massive extortion racket and laundering over ₹70 crore through a web of 'benami' bank accounts

title: Centre willing to proceed with Hyderabad Metro Rail Phase II on 50:50 cost-sharing basis: Khattar informs Kishan Reddy
content: Under the Centre’s AMRUT 2.0 scheme, 39 sewage treatment plants (STPs) were sanctioned to treat wastewater flowing into the Musi, says Union Minister for Housing and Urban Affairs

title: EC doubles security arrangements for Falta repoll in West Bengal
content: According to an official at the state Chief Electoral Officer, eight Central Armed Police Force (CAPF) personnel, equivalent to one full section, will be deployed at each polling booth

title: Class 10 Board exam results: Tamil Nadu records 94.3% pass rate
content: While Pudukottai topped the State with a pass percentage of 97.57%, Sivaganga came second with 97.54%, followed by Thanjavur at 97.41%

title: ‘No place for personal enmity in politics’, says G. Sudhakaran on swearing in former Kerala CM Pinarayi as MLA
content: Sudhakaran, who has been sworn in as Pro Tem Speaker of Kerala Assembly, says he sees ‘no difference’ between Pinarayi Vijayan and any other MLA to whom he will be administering the oath of office on Thursday

title: No uniform definition of liquor ‘bottle’; SC seeks response from Centre, States
content: The court said lack of a clear definition has led to ‘very deceptive’ packaging of cheap alcohol, with liquor even passed off as ‘fruit juice’; some excise laws include sacks and wrappers as ‘bottle’

title: One-day nationwide chemists' strike begins on mixed note
content: AIOCD General Secretary Rajiv Singhal reiterated that AIOCD is protesting the GSR 817 and GSR 220 notifications, as these are effectively regularising online pharmacies.

title: Chemists in A.P. respond to bandh call, down the shutters of medical shops
content: Druggists say e-pharmacies let people buy habit-forming drugs, antibiotics and pregnancy-termination kits without the prescription that chemist shops require

title: Philanthropy Asia Summit: Indian rural-focused start-ups showcase growth by promoting livelihood of farmers
content: More than 30 start-ups from across Asia showcased their growth stories at the ongoing Philanthropy Asia Summit

title: Nothing wrong in holding caste census, any govt of the day should know who is backward, SC says
content: The Chief Justice said it was not within the court’s domain to decide whether a caste enumeration should necessarily be a part or not of Census 2027

title: TVK extends open invitation to VCK, IUML to be part of Tamil Nadu Cabinet
content: “We invite VCK leader Thol. Thirumavalavan to be part of the Council of Ministers. This is the wish of the Chief Minister as well. Similarly, the IUML should also join the Council of Ministers. We have conveyed our invitation to IUML leaders,” Minister Aadhav Arjuna told reporters in Chennai.

title: Defamation FIR lodged over social media posts on U.P. Minister's convoy size in Bhadohi
content: Police alleged that the viral social media posts were intended to tarnish the image of the government and administration and mislead the public

title: Great Nicobar project is purely commercial with no military infrastructure element: Congress
content: Jairam Ramesh accused the Centre of pursuing what he termed a policy of “Continuing, Calibrated Capitulation to China”

title: Heavy discounts, old prescriptions and 10-minute deliveries: Why thousands of pharmacies in Telangana are going on strike today
content: The strike call was given by the All India Organisation of Chemists and Druggists (AIOCD), raising concerns over unfair practices by online pharmacies and corporate medicine retail chains

title: Trouble mounts for Abhishek Banerjee as Kolkata civic body sends notice against his properties
content: In the last few days, Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari has been talking about properties owned by the Trinamool Congress general secretary and other Trinamool leaders that are under scrutiny

title: Modi and Meloni outline road map for India-Italy strategic partnership
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and Italian Premier Giorgia Meloni joint op-ed talks about emerging “Indo-Mediterranean” corridor and pushes for €20-billion bilateral trade target by 2029.

title: Who is a Pro-tem Speaker, and why is the role crucial for a new Assembly?
content: The Pro-tem Speaker serves as the essential bridge of a new Assembly, anchoring the legislative process by administering oaths and steering the House toward the election of its permanent Speaker

title: Chemists strike on May 20: Reasons behind today’s all-India strike
content: While major pharmacy chains and government medical outlets will remain operational, the strike highlights significant tensions within the industry that could impact access to medications nationwide.

title: Karnataka Police orders State-wide inspection drive to streamline policing
content: ADGPs and IGPs have been assigned districts and commissionerates to review policing standards, administrative efficiency and implementation of new criminal laws between May and July 2026.

title: How Karunanidhi successfully headed a minority government in Tamil Nadu for five years
content: The last time Tamil Nadu witnessed a coalition government was in 1952; however, leaders of the Dravidian parties largely steered clear of the idea thereafter

title: G. Sudhakaran sworn in as Kerala Assembly Pro Tem Speaker
content: Appointment of Sudhakaran, who won from Ambalappuzha constituency with UDF backing after a high-profile rebellion against his former party, CPI(M), adheres to established parliamentary convention of designating the senior-most member of the House to the temporary post

title: KDA objects to teaching three languages in CBSE schools
content: The Union Education Ministry has made the study of three languages (R1, R2, R3) mandatory for class 9 and 10 in Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) schools with effect from July 1

title: Security forces carry out search operation in Manipur to trace those held hostage by armed groups
content: More than 38 people were abducted and held hostage by armed groups in Kangpokpi and Senapati districts on May 13

title: Andhra Pradesh attracted ₹23 lakh crore investments under NDA, says CM Naidu
content: Royal Enfield plant planned for Tirupati; defence, drone, space and electronics units to come up in Rayalaseema

title: Nellore emerging as industrial hub with ₹10,000 crore investment: Chandrababu Naidu
content: Naidu announces roads connecting 25 fishing villages at a cost of ₹6.27 crore, desilting of North Buckingham Canal with ₹6.19 crore; seven industries to invest ₹10,000 crore in the district

title: Bandh call: Pharmacies to stay open on May 20, but chemists to show support by sporting black badges and ribbons
content: “Many online portals are announcing excessive discounts even on life-saving medicines and attracting the public through social media and advertisements; this is sending the wrong message,” the MCDRCA said

title: Dairy farmers ask government to hike price of Aavin milk
content: Tamil Nadu Milk Producers Welfare Association general secretary M.G. Rajendran said the TVK, in its election manifesto, had promised ₹44 per litre of milk

title: Shyam Metalics announces ₹20,000 crore investment push in Bengal
content: Company plans railway manufacturing unit in Kharagpur, expansion of workforce to 45,000; seeks easier land policy and faster industrial growth under new government

title: Age, Height Mismatch: The Unanswered Questions In Twisha Sharma Case
content: Twisha Sharma's death came barely five months after her marriage to Samarth Singh, a lawyer. The victim was 33 years old.

title: Chopper Carrying Delhi Family Hits Power Line, Lands In Field In Uttarakhand
content: According to reports, a technical issue developed in the Trans Bharat Aviation company's helicopter while it was flying over the Chamba-Arakot area around 8.30 am.

title: 'They Don't Have Parents': Woman Elopes With Lover, Leaves Children On A Bus
content: The woman seated her two children on the Pandharpur-Sambhaji Nagar bus and departed with her lover on a scooter she had stolen from her father.

title: US Government To Drop Tax Claims Against Trump After Lawsuit Settlement
content: The move comes after the Trump administration announced Monday, as part of the lawsuit settlement, the creation of a nearly $1.8 billion fund to compensate allies of the Republican president.

title: Meta, LinkedIn Layoffs: Is This The End Of 'Dream Job' Era?
content: Life decisions are on hold. Employees say they are unable to plan a future because they don't know if they will still have a salary next quarter.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Twisha Sharma: Why India's Wealthiest Women Are Still Dying In 'Respectable' Marriages
content: Data show that women belonging to the lowest and highest wealth quintiles have equally been exposed to physical violence. Clearly, educational backgrounds and social status are no bar.

title: Republican Leader Who Stood Up To Trump Defeated In Kentucky Primary
content: Massie has angered Trump by opposing US military action in Iran and Venezuela, criticising aid to Israel, resisting parts of the president's agenda and helping push for the release of files related to multimillionaire sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

title: Reel Of The Day: PM's Special 'Melody' Gift To Meloni And A Viral Moment
content: Internet users often use the "Melodi" hashtag to describe the friendly equation between the two leaders.

title: 'Keep Stray Dogs In Colleges If You Accept Liability': Supreme Court To Students' Bodies
content: The Supreme Court on Tuesday had allowed authorities to carry out euthanasia of rabid and aggressive stray dogs.

title: In Big Move, Kerala Government Scraps LDF's Ambitious High Speed Rail Project
content: The cabinet also decided to extend the validity of the public service commission's rank lists till November 30.

title: On Camera, Hyderabad Teen Lifts Woman's Dress, Runs Away, Probe On
content: The woman was returning from school with her child in her left arm when a teenager approached her from behind, and lifted her dress.

title: Southwest Airlines Launches Global Innovation Centre In Hyderabad
content: Southwest Airlines launches its first global innovation centre in Hyderabad to boost tech and engineering operations.

title: Google Unveils "Audio Glasses", Taking on Meta's Ray-Ban Smart Spectacles
content: The Google's so-called "audio glasses" will be equipped with a microphone, camera and small speaker, and will allow users to make calls, listen to music, take photos and chat with the Gemini AI assistant.
